title: HTTP Semantics, Messages, Versions, and the Wire Model · Language Lens: Method Status Body Semantics
summary: A raw implementation copies endpoint behavior into its parser, confuses resources with representations, infers message bodies from method names, and treats HTTP/1.1 syntax as universal HTTP semantics. Planned artifact: Add and verify the method status body semantics increment in version-neutral message and semantics model. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Determine content meaning and permission from request method, response status, request context, and connection mode rather than one field alone.
Model control data, header fields, content, trailer fields, and message metadata without inventing content where semantics forbid it.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Predict Method Status Body Semantics · Theory Method Status Body Semantics
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory protocol team handles case 8485 in a protocol upgrade gateway transferring buffered tunnel bytes. Build deterministic Go evidence to derive message content from method and status semantics before wire framing; preserve this operating constraint: one connection has exactly one read owner and one terminal close owner; then replace one field with repeated non-combinable lines and preserve their order. Browser code models bounded byte and state decisions only. Sockets, listeners, live TLS, proxies, packet capture, fuzz campaigns, races, load, HTTP/2, HTTP/3, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.
